title: Enforce atomic public release publication
outOfScope: installer transactionality and compatibility probe behavior
production repository settings or third-party attestations
new version selection and production release execution

sources.reason: Task 3 requires atomic public publication ordering and fail-closed release gates.
Current workflow creates the tag and draft Release before the final immutable-tag smoke verification.
Existing workflow tests assert exact-SHA and draft-release ordering.
Distribution contract documents release publication ordering and trust boundaries.

riskAssessment.reason: A partially published tag or Release must not be presented as verified when the final immutable-tag smoke gate fails.

executionDecision.reason: The workflow ordering and failure behavior are testable locally and explicitly authorized by the user.

preReviewWarnings: Verify that no tag or Release is created before every source-bound and immutable-tag gate passes.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Release evidence is generated and validated before any public tag or GitHub Release mutation.
The workflow verifies the immutable tag and final smoke gate before publishing the Release, and failure remains fail-closed.
Tests assert the complete ordering and prevent regressions to draft/public publication sequencing.
English and Japanese distribution references document the atomic publication boundary.
guidelines: Keep all release mutations after source-bound and immutable-tag verification gates.
Do not weaken exact-SHA, existing-tag, smoke, compatibility, or release-asset identity checks.
Document the publication state machine in both distribution references.

intent.problem: The workflow creates the tag and draft Release before dispatching the strict immutable-tag smoke verification, so a later failure can leave public release state that was never fully verified.
constraints: Keep the existing exact-SHA and source-bound evidence gates.
Do not add destructive cleanup that could delete an existing immutable tag.
Use tests to prove ordering without making network mutations.
intent.rationale: Publication must be the final mutation after all evidence and immutable-tag behavior gates succeed.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: All validation gates precede tag and Release creation.
scenarioCoverage.reason: Workflow ordering tests will assert the sequence.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Immutable-tag smoke failure prevents Release publication.
scenarioCoverage.reason: The workflow must place publication after the strict smoke step.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Existing exact-SHA and release asset identity checks remain present.
scenarioCoverage.reason: Existing workflow regression tests will remain green.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the complete remediation, PR merge, and release workflow.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
